item.title: Update to the PMHC MDS - 1/7/2026 (v3.3.4)
item.description:
PMHC MDS Update v3.3.4

We are pleased to announce that PMHC MDS Update v3.3.4 was released on July 01, 2026.

A reminder that the current version of the core data specification is PMHC Version 5.0.

The currently supported data specifications are documented at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/data-specifications.html#current-versions

The PMHC MDS will no longer accept uploads using the PMHC Version 4.1 or The Way Back Version 3.0 Extension formats. An error will be returned if a file is uploaded in either of these formats.

The following updates have been applied in the reporting interface:

  • The following report has been updated to allow grouping down to site level:
    • M7 — Monthly service activity
  • The following report has been updated to allow grouping without site level:
    • M8 — Episode of care characteristics (The M8 is also being updated to allow grouping down to site level and will be released shortly)

The user guides have been updated to reflect these updates. They are available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/.

item.title: Final reminder - End of life for PMHC MDS v4.1 and Way Back v3.0 specifications
item.description:
The current version of the core PMHC MDS data specification is Version 5.0. This has been available for upload since the 18th of December 2025.

Since the 18th of December 2026 there has been a transition period allowing for uploads in PMHC MDS Version 4.1 and The Way Back Version 3.0 extension formats. This transition period is intended to give organisations time to understand and implement the change.

After the 30th of June 2026 the PMHC MDS will no longer accept uploads using the Version 4.1 or The Way Back Version 3.0 Extension formats. If you are able to upload in Version 5.0 now, please start using this format immediately.

Note: There is no requirement imposed by the system to wait until the 30th of June 2026 before supplying in Version 5.0 format.

Please contact support@pmhc-mds.com if you will not be able to upload in the PMHC MDS Version 5.0 format by 30th of June 2026.

PMHC MDS data specifications are documented at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/data-specifications.html#current-versions

A summary of the changes between version 4.1 and 5.0 is available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/projects/data-specification/en/v5.0/upgrading-from-previous-versions.html.

item.title: Update to the PMHC MDS - 19/6/2026 (v3.3.3)
item.description:
PMHC MDS Update v3.3.3

We are pleased to announce that PMHC MDS Update v3.3.3 was released on June 19, 2026.

The following updates have been applied in the reporting interface:

  • Fixed a bug where the M5 and M6 reports returned a blank screen

The following updates have been applied to the upload and data entry interfaces:

  • Fixed a bug where Organisation Type referred to at Intake Conclusion with a value of ‘44: HeadtoHelp / HeadtoHealth’ or ‘42: AMHC’ was being prevented from being retained on existing data.

A bug was also fixed that prevented some nightly generated emails from being sent.

The user guides have been updated to reflect these updates. They are available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/.

item.title: Reminder of the End of Life for PMHC MDS Version 4.1 Data Specification and The Way Back Version 3.0 Data Specifications
item.description:
The PMHC MDS currently supports uploads in the following data specifications:

  • PMHC Version 4.1
  • PMHC Version 5.0
  • The Way Back Version 3.0 Extension

These are documented at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/data-specifications.html#current-versions

After the 30th of June 2026 the PMHC MDS will no longer accept uploads using the Version 4.1 or The Way Back Version 3.0 Extension formats. Please ensure that your systems have been migrated to using the Version 5.0 upload format before this date.

A summary of the changes between version 4.1 and 5.0 is available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/projects/data-specification/en/v5.0/upgrading-from-previous-versions.html.

item.title: Update to the PMHC MDS - 10/6/2026 (v3.3.0)
item.description:
PMHC MDS Update v3.3.0

We are pleased to announce that PMHC MDS Update v3.3.0 was released on June 10, 2026.

The following updates have been applied in the reporting interface:

  • The following reports have been updated to allow grouping down to site level:
    • M1 — Client wait time
    • M2 — Activity Reports by Age group
    • M3 — Activity Reports by Gender
    • M4 — Activity Reports by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander status
    • M5 — IAR-DST - Recommended Level of Care
    • M6 — IAR-DST - Recommended Level of Care, where intake referred to MMHC
    • M9 — Activity Report: Service contact type
    • M10 — Activity Report: Service contact duration
  • The M1 has also been redefined to calculate client wait time as the number of days between referral date as recorded on the episode record and the date of the first contact, whether attended or not. The documentation has been updated to reflect this.
  • The following reports have temporarily been taken offline. They are also being updated to allow grouping down to site level and will be released shortly.
    • M7 — Monthly service activity
    • M8 — Episode of care characteristics
  • The W1 report has been updated so that the Safety Plan and Support Plan KPIs are more compatible with other indicators. Since its introduction as part of The Way Back trial, these KPIs have been based on the number of Safety and Support Plans recorded (that is, ‘collection occasions’). On review, it has been determined that a more appropriate basis for these KPIs is the number of episodes that include at least one Safety or Support Plan. The presence of at least one Safety or Support Plan within an episode is sufficient to meet the KPI.

The following updates have been applied in the user management interface:

  • Fixed a bug stopping users with the user management role from seeing the user audit table.

The user guides have been updated to reflect these updates. They are available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/.

item.title: Update to the PMHC MDS - 15/5/2026 (v3.2.5)
item.description:
PMHC MDS Update v3.2.5

We are pleased to announce that PMHC MDS Update v3.2.5 was released on May 15, 2026.

The following updates have been applied in the user management interface:

  • User search and listing of users such as in the user audit emails, now excludes users who have no roles at relevant organisations.

The following updates have been applied in the login interface:

  • Fixed a bug where roles were not being assigned to new users.

The user guides have been updated to reflect these updates. They are available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/.

item.title: Reminder of the End of Life for PMHC MDS Version 4.1 Data Specification and The Way Back Version 3.0 Data Specifications
item.description:
The PMHC MDS currently supports uploads in the following data specifications:

  • PMHC Version 4.1
  • PMHC Version 5.0
  • The Way Back Version 3.0 Extension

These are documented at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/data-specifications.html#current-versions

After the 30th of June 2026 the PMHC MDS will no longer accept uploads using the Version 4.1 or The Way Back Version 3.0 Extension formats. Please ensure that your systems have been migrated to using the Version 5.0 upload format before this date.

A summary of the changes between version 4.1 and 5.0 is available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/projects/data-specification/en/v5.0/upgrading-from-previous-versions.html.

item.title: Update to the PMHC MDS - 8/5/2026 (v3.2.4)
item.description:
PMHC MDS Update v3.2.4

We are pleased to announce that PMHC MDS Update v3.2.4 was released on May 8, 2026.

The following updates have been applied in the upload interface:

  • Fixed a bug where emails were not being sent after a successful upload.
  • Improved the error returned when the episode_tags column is missing in an episodes worksheet/file.

The following updates have been applied in the login interface:

  • Fixed a bug where new users couldn’t log in after accepting their invite.

The user guides have been updated to reflect these updates. They are available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/.

item.title: Update to the PMHC MDS - 4/5/2026 (v3.2.2)
item.description:
PMHC MDS Update v3.2.2

We are pleased to announce that PMHC MDS Update v3.2.2 was released on May 4, 2026.

As per the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing’s (DoHDA) Circular on 30 April, 2026 which is published at https://pmhc-mds.com/communications/#/2026/04/30/FOR-ACTION-New-MMHC-system-tags-in-the-PMHC-MDS/ the tags !rhcpsychology and !rhcpsychiatry are now available through Data Entry, Upload and Data Extracts in the PMHC MDS.

Version 5.0.3 of the base PMHC MDS data specification has been released to add additional validations to support these tags.

Please refer to DoHDA’s Circular linked above for details about usage of these tags.

The following updates have been applied in the data entry interface:

  • Fixed a bug in the add client form that could stop navigation away from the unsaved data modal.

The following updates have been applied in the reporting interface:

  • Updated the table headers in the A16 report to clarify what each column is counting.

The following updates have been applied in the data extract interface:

  • Fixed a bug in table pagination where the number of rows displayed would not change to more than 10.

The user guides have been updated to reflect these updates. They are available at https://docs.pmhc-mds.com/.

item.title: FOR INFORMATION: PMHC MDS transition to the AIHW – contact information
item.description:
The below circular was sent to PHN CEOs on Thursday 30 April 2026.

-----------------------

Dear PHN CEOs,

As part of the transition of the management of the Primary Mental Health Care Minimum Data Set (PMHC MDS) from the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing (the department) to the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare (AIHW), the department is providing updated contact information to support PHNs during this change.

Please refer to the attached document for additional detail.

For any queries please contact PMHC.MDS@aihw.gov.au.

Kind regards,

Mental Health Data

PHN CEO Circular

Primary Mental Health Care Minimum Data Set Transition to the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare – Contact Information

As part of the transition of the management of the Primary Mental Health Care Minimum Data Set (PMHC MDS) from the Department of Health, Disability and Ageing (the department) to the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare (AIHW), the department is providing updated contact information to support PHNs during this change. AIHW is an independent statutory government agency that manages key national data collections and reports on health and welfare data for the Australian community, including in the mental health area.

Many PHNs will already be familiar with the work they have been progressing over the past year, including re‑establishing the PMHC Data Reference Group (PMHC DRG) and conducting a comprehensive review of the PMHC MDS. This review has involved extensive consultation with PHNs, provider organisations, system vendors and other key stakeholders. The insights gathered through this process will help shape future improvements to the dataset.

The transition of PMHC MDS management is progressing smoothly. AIHW is steadily taking on additional roles and responsibilities and has developed a detailed understanding of the technical, operational and governance elements of the PMHC MDS. This staged approach positions AIHW well to support PHNs and to guide ongoing system improvements.

PMHC MDS related support

To support the transition, AIHW has established a dedicated PMHC MDS team inbox (pmhc.mds@aihw.gov.au). Please direct all data-related queries to this inbox in the first instance. The AIHW team will be your primary point of contact moving forward and will coordinate with the department as required.

This includes queries such as advice on whether particular programs, activities or funding streams are in scope for PMHC MDS data collection, queries about the data model, clarification of how programs should be represented or categorised within the PMHC MDS, questions about mapping local program arrangements to PMHC MDS program types or service activity, and guidance on how changes to programs or service models may affect PMHC MDS reporting. The AIHW will also continue to manage the PMHC DRG.

The department will continue to oversee governance and policy matters relating to the PMHC MDS and will work closely with AIHW to ensure a smooth and well-supported transition for PHNs.

PHN MHSP funding related queries

Please continue to direct queries related to PHN mental health and suicide prevention program funding to the Mental Health and Suicide Prevention Division PHN Program and Policy team inbox (MHPHNRPC@Health.gov.au).

PMHC MDS technical system support

Logicly’s PMHC Help Desk (support@pmhc-mds.com) remains available to support PHNs with technical system matters as per current arrangements. This includes:

  • Assistance with system access and user accounts
  • Data upload and submission issues
  • Deletion or suppression of inactive provider organisation information
  • Validation or error messages generated by the PMHC MDS system
  • System functionality or performance issues
  • Technical user training, including user management, data upload and data entry
  • Troubleshooting technical problems encountered when using the PMHC MDS platform.
